weapons.name: Pistol
weapons.traitEffect: Easy to conceal, common backup weapon.

weapons.name: Rifle
weapons.traitEffect: No special trait — consistent and dependable.

weapons.name: Blade
weapons.traitEffect: Can only be used in base contact.

weapons.name: Sniper Rifle
weapons.traitEffect: +1 attack die when shooting beyond range band, instead of the usual penalty.

weapons.name: LMG
weapons.traitEffect: Target suffers -2" movement on their next activation.

weapons.name: Launcher
weapons.traitEffect: All models within 2" of the target point take hits.

weapons.name: Tactical Knife
weapons.traitEffect: A heavy combat blade that finds gaps in armor. Target's health die shifts an extra step on failed check.

abilities.name: Hardened
abilities.effect: +1 step to GRT die for recovery and morale checks.

abilities.name: CQB Expert
abilities.effect: Gains a bonus attack die in brawls.

abilities.name: Marksman
abilities.effect: Gains a bonus attack die when shooting a target within range band.

abilities.name: Medic
abilities.effect: Use Gear action: roll TEC — on success, attempt recovery on a Down ally in base contact.

abilities.name: Hacker
abilities.effect: Gains a bonus die on TEC checks made for scenario interactions.

abilities.name: Networked
abilities.effect: Once per round, one ally within 6" may re-roll a single failed AWR check.

abilities.name: Stealthy
abilities.effect: Enemies beyond 12" must pass an AWR check before targeting this operative.

abilities.name: Spotter
abilities.effect: Once per round, designate a target: all friendly shooters gain +1 attack die vs. that target this round.

abilities.name: Resilient
abilities.effect: The first failed health check each game is treated as a pass (health die still degrades one step).

abilities.name: Tactician
abilities.effect: Once per round, swap activation order with one friendly operative.

abilities.name: Rapid Assault
abilities.effect: After a successful Fight action, may immediately move up to 3" (no additional action required).

abilities.name: Pinning Fire
abilities.effect: Shoot action: instead of dealing damage, force the target to pass a GRT check or lose their next action.

abilities.name: Breacher
abilities.effect: Ignores the cover penalty when shooting.

abilities.name: Field Repair
abilities.effect: Use Gear action: roll TEC — on success, restore one health step to a friendly operative in base contact.

abilities.name: Ghost
abilities.effect: When hidden, enemies must pass an AWR check at -1 die to spot this operative.

abilities.name: Heavy Weapons
abilities.effect: May carry one premium weapon without spending a gear slot.

abilities.name: Commander
abilities.effect: All friendly operatives within 6" gain +1 to GRT for morale checks.

behaviorTags.name: Aggressive
behaviorTags.personality: Closes distance aggressively, prefers melee over shooting. Gets into combat as fast as possible.

table.action: Move toward nearest visible enemy. Fight if in base contact.
Move toward nearest enemy. Shoot if in line of sight.
Full move toward nearest enemy. Fight if in contact; otherwise Shoot.

behaviorTags.name: Defensive
behaviorTags.personality: Holds position, shoots from cover. Prioritizes staying behind cover and engaging enemies at range.

table.action: If in cover: Shoot nearest visible enemy. If not in cover: move to nearest available cover.
Hold position. Shoot nearest visible enemy.
Move to better cover within 6". Shoot nearest visible enemy if possible.

behaviorTags.name: Cautious
behaviorTags.personality: Avoids risk and repositions frequently. Maintains maximum range and uses cover to survive.

table.action: Move away from nearest enemy to maintain range. Shoot if able.
Move toward nearest objective. Interact if in contact.
Move to cover with best sight lines. Shoot nearest visible enemy.

behaviorTags.name: Flanker
behaviorTags.personality: Seeks angles and avoids the frontline. Moves to the sides or rear of enemies to attack from unexpected angles.

table.action: Move toward the flank or rear of the nearest enemy. Shoot if in line of sight.
If behind cover with line of sight to enemy rear: Shoot. Otherwise reposition to a better angle.
Move to the closest enemy not currently engaged by a friendly. Shoot or Fight as appropriate.

scenarios.name: Blackout
scenarios.briefing: Seize and hold data relay points in a blacked-out district. Control the nodes and lock out the opposition.
scenarios.deployment: Both players deploy within 6" of their respective table edges (opposite long edges).
scenarios.objectives: Place 3 Data Relay markers along the center line: one in the exact center, one 8" left of center, one 8" right of center.
specialRules: Blackout Conditions: All shooting beyond 12" suffers an additional -1 die penalty (stacks with range/cover penalties).
Relays are controlled by having at least one operative within 3" and no enemies within 3".
vpConditions: Control a Data Relay at end of round = 1 VP per relay
Control all 3 relays simultaneously at end of any round = bonus 2 VP

scenarios.name: Scorched Data
scenarios.briefing: Race to hack or destroy enemy terminals before they do the same to yours. Every second counts.
scenarios.deployment: Players deploy in diagonally opposite corners, within a 8" triangle measured from the corner point.
scenarios.objectives: Place 4 Data Terminal markers, one in the center of each table quarter.
specialRules: Hack Terminal: Interact action using TEC. Roll 2 TEC dice — 1+ success = hacked.
Once hacked, a terminal is locked and cannot be re-hacked by the opponent.
If an enemy is within 3" of your hacked terminal at end of round, it becomes contested (no VP that round).
vpConditions: Control a hacked terminal at end of round (no enemies within 3") = 1 VP

scenarios.name: Dead Drop
scenarios.briefing: Retrieve intel packages scattered across the map and exfil them to your deployment zone. The enemy wants the same packages.
scenarios.deployment: Both players deploy within 6" of their respective table edges (opposite long edges).
scenarios.objectives: Place 4 Intel Package markers in the center band of the table — each at least 6" from any table edge and at least 6" from each other.
specialRules: Pick Up: Interact action (no roll needed) while in base contact with an unclaimed package.
Carrying: An operative carrying a package has -2" movement. They can only carry 1 package at a time.
Drop: If the carrier goes Down, the package drops at their location. Any operative can pick it up.
Reveal: When a package is picked up, roll d6 to reveal its value: 1-3 = Standard Intel (2 VP on delivery), 4-5 = Priority Intel (3 VP on delivery), 6 = Decoy (0 VP on delivery).
Deliver: Move the carrying operative into your deployment zone to score.

scenarios.name: Signal Jam
scenarios.briefing: Hold the central transmitter for 3 consecutive rounds to broadcast your signal and deny the enemy theirs.
scenarios.deployment: Both players deploy within 6" of their respective table edges (opposite long edges).
scenarios.objectives: Place 1 Transmitter marker in the exact center of the table.
specialRules: Control: The transmitter is controlled by having more operatives within 3" than the enemy at end of round.
Broadcast Counter: Track consecutive rounds of control. If one player controls the transmitter for 3 consecutive rounds, they win immediately.
Losing control resets your broadcast counter to 0.
Interference Zone: All shooting by operatives within 6" of the transmitter suffers -1 die (signal interference).
vpConditions: Control transmitter for 3 consecutive rounds = Immediate Victory
Control transmitter at end of round = 1 VP
If no one achieves broadcast victory, highest VP at end of round 6 wins

scenarios.name: Turf War
scenarios.briefing: The district is up for grabs. Five zones, one winner. Control territory and push the enemy out.
scenarios.deployment: Both players deploy within 6" of their respective table edges (opposite long edges).
scenarios.objectives: Divide the table into 5 zones: 4 quarters plus a central zone (6" radius from center). Place a zone marker in the center of each.
specialRules: Zones are controlled by having more operatives inside the zone than the enemy at end of round.
The central zone is worth double VP.
An operative can only count for one zone (if overlapping, controlling player chooses).
vpConditions: Control outer zone at end of round = 1 VP
Control central zone at end of round = 2 VP

scenarios.name: Headhunter
scenarios.briefing: Intel says they have high-value targets. Find them, eliminate them, and send a message.
scenarios.deployment: Both players deploy within 6" of their respective table edges (opposite long edges).
scenarios.objectives: Place 2 objective markers on the center line, each 8" from center (left and right). These are intel caches.
specialRules: Priority Targets: Eliminating the enemy Leader = 3 VP (instead of the usual 2). Eliminating a Specialist = 2 VP (instead of 1).
Intel Cache: Interact action at a cache marker. Roll AWR — on success, you learn the positions of all hidden/stealthy enemies (negates Stealthy and Ghost for the rest of the round).
First Blood: The first player to eliminate an enemy operative gains +1 bonus VP.
vpConditions: Control intel cache at end of game = 1 VP

scenarios.name: Crash Site
scenarios.briefing: A cargo drone just went down in the middle of the district. Both teams want what's inside. Grab the cargo and get it home.
scenarios.deployment: Both players deploy within 6" of their respective table edges (opposite long edges).
scenarios.objectives: Place 1 Crash Site marker in the exact center. Place 3 Cargo tokens on the crash site.
specialRules: Recover Cargo: Interact action at the crash site to pick up 1 cargo token. One per activation.
Carrying: An operative carrying cargo has -2" movement. Can only carry 1 token.
Drop: Cargo drops if carrier goes Down.
Deliver: Move carrying operative into your deployment zone to score. Cargo is removed from play once delivered.
The crash site is dangerous terrain — any operative ending activation within 2" of it rolls 1d6: on a 1, take 1 hit (fuel leak).
vpConditions: Deliver cargo to your deployment zone = 2 VP per token
All 3 cargo delivered by one side = bonus 2 VP

scenarios.name: Extraction
scenarios.briefing: Locate and extract a VIP from a hostile zone before the enemy closes in. Time is against you.
scenarios.deployment: Player deploys within 6" of one table edge. Enemy/AI operatives deploy scattered across the opposite half of the table (at least 12" from the player's deployment edge).
scenarios.objectives: Place 1 VIP marker in the exact center of the table.
specialRules: The VIP is a neutral figure (d6 health, no attacks). Any operative in base contact can move the VIP with them (uses the operative's full movement).
If the VIP goes Down, they remain in place. Any operative can attempt to revive (Interact action, TEC check).
The VIP exits the table at the player's deployment edge for victory points.
vpConditions: Extract VIP off player's deployment edge = 5 VP

scenarios.name: Sweep & Clear
scenarios.briefing: Eliminate all hostiles in a building complex. Designed for solo and co-op play against the AI.
scenarios.deployment: Player deploys within 6" of one table edge. AI operatives are divided evenly among the 4 table zones and placed by the player (representing hidden positions — at least 6" from the player's deployment edge).
scenarios.objectives: The table is divided into 4 equal zones (2x2 grid). Clear each zone of all hostiles.
specialRules: A zone is cleared when no active (non-Down, non-Out) enemy operatives remain in it.
Reinforcements: At the end of rounds 2 and 4, roll d6. On a 4+, one AI Recruit (Aggressive) enters from a random non-player table edge (roll d6: 1-2 left, 3-4 right, 5-6 opposite).
Solo/Co-op Focus: This scenario is designed for solo play or co-op against the AI. For PvP, one player controls the defenders.
vpConditions: Clear a zone = 2 VP per zone
All 4 zones cleared = bonus 3 VP (total victory)

scenarios.name: Holdout
scenarios.briefing: They know where you are and they're coming. Dig in, hold your ground, and survive until extraction arrives.
scenarios.deployment: Player deploys within 6" of the table center. AI operatives deploy along all four table edges (within 3" of edge).
scenarios.objectives: Place 1 Holdout Point marker in the exact center. The player must maintain control at the end of round 6.
specialRules: Wave System: The AI warband starts with a basic force. At the end of rounds 2, 4, and 6, reinforcements arrive — roll d6 per reinforcement wave: 1-2 = 2 Recruits (Aggressive), 3-4 = 1 Operative + 1 Recruit (Aggressive), 5-6 = 1 Specialist + 1 Recruit (mixed).
Reinforcement entry: Roll d6 for each new operative: 1=North, 2=East, 3=South, 4=West, 5-6=closest edge to the Holdout Point.
Surrounded: AI operatives always count as having line of sight for target priority (they know where you are).
vpConditions: Survive to end of round 6 with at least 1 operative standing = 5 VP
Control Holdout Point at end of round 6 = 3 VP

scenarios.name: Data Heist
scenarios.briefing: A corporate server farm sits in the heart of enemy territory. Get in, hack the data, and get out before they lock you down.
scenarios.deployment: Player deploys within 6" of one table edge. AI operatives deploy in the opposite half, concentrated around 2 Server Terminal markers.
scenarios.objectives: Place 2 Server Terminal markers in the opposite half of the table, each at least 8" from the player's deployment edge and 6" apart.
specialRules: Hack Server: Interact action at a terminal. Roll TEC — 2+ successes = hacked. 1 success = partial (need another Interact action to finish). 0 = security alert triggered.
Security Alert: If triggered (0 successes on hack, or an AI operative spots a player within 6" of a terminal), all AI operatives gain +1 die to CMB for the rest of the game (heightened security).
Exfiltration: After hacking both servers, player operatives must exit via their deployment edge to secure the data.

Stealth Phase: AI operatives do not activate until a player operative is spotted (within line of sight and 12" of an AI operative, or a failed hack triggers alert). Until then, AI operatives remain stationary.
vpConditions: Exfil at least 1 operative after both hacks = 3 VP
No security alert triggered = bonus 2 VP

scenarios.name: Ambush
scenarios.briefing: Your patrol walked right into a trap. Hostiles on all sides. Fight your way to the extraction point before they close the net.
scenarios.deployment: Player deploys within 4" of the table center (patrol starting position). AI operatives deploy along all four table edges, within 4" of edges.
scenarios.objectives: Place 1 Extraction Point marker on a randomly determined table edge (roll d6: 1-2 North, 3-4 East, 5 South, 6 West).
specialRules: Ambush: AI has initiative automatically on round 1 (no roll).
Closing Net: From round 3 onward, at the end of each round, all AI operatives that didn't activate this round may move 3" toward the nearest player operative (the net tightens).
Extraction: A player operative in contact with the Extraction Point may exit the table as their action. Exited operatives are safe.
Urgency: If no player operative has exited by end of round 4, the extraction point moves 6" toward center (extraction window shrinking).
vpConditions: Extract operative (exit at extraction point) = 2 VP each
Extract more than half your warband = bonus 3 VP
Player operative goes Out before extraction = -1 VP

scenarios.name: Bug Hunt
scenarios.briefing: Something's in the building complex. Room by room, floor by floor — find them before they find you.
scenarios.deployment: Player deploys within 6" of one table edge. AI operatives are placed face-down (hidden) by the player across the rest of the table — at least one per zone.
scenarios.objectives: Divide the table into 6 zones (3x2 grid). AI operatives are hidden in each zone.
specialRules: Hidden Enemies: AI operatives start face-down (hidden). A player operative entering a zone with hidden enemies must roll AWR — on 1+ success, reveal all hidden enemies in that zone. On 0 successes, the hidden enemies get a free activation (ambush) before being revealed.
Ambush Activation: Revealed enemies via failed AWR check immediately activate (one each) before normal play resumes.
Sweep: A zone is considered cleared when all enemies in it are Out.
Close Quarters: All ranges reduced — weapon range bands halved (round up). The table is tight.
vpConditions: Reveal enemies without triggering ambush = 1 VP per zone

aiProfiles.name: Street Wolves
aiProfiles.description: A large street gang that overwhelms with numbers. Cheap bodies, aggressive tactics, led by a veteran enforcer.

aiProfiles.name: Obsidian Corp Security
aiProfiles.description: Professional corporate security team. Disciplined, well-equipped, holds ground and picks targets methodically.

aiProfiles.name: Ghost Protocol
aiProfiles.description: Elite black ops team. Few in number but highly skilled. Strikes from unexpected angles and disappears.

aiProfiles.name: Iron Jackals
aiProfiles.description: Brutal close-combat gang that rushes the enemy and overwhelms them in melee. Led by a terrifying pit fighter.

aiProfiles.name: Neon Shadows
aiProfiles.description: Tech-savvy infiltration team. Prefers to hack, sneak, and outmaneuver rather than engage in straight firefights.

aiProfiles.name: Razorback Militia
aiProfiles.description: Dug-in territorial militia. Fortifies positions and punishes anyone who comes too close. Patient and disciplined.
